entry.title: Navigating Central Procurement: Your Map to Integrated Success
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, streamlining procurement processes can mean the difference between success and stagnation. With the integration of SAP S/4HANA into your system landscape, you can achieve centralized procurement processes that span multiple ERP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP ERP. This powerful central procurement functionality enables your organization to operate more efficiently and effectively.

However, the wealth of features and processes within Central Procurement can sometimes feel overwhelming. From central contract distribution to Ariba integration scenarios, there are numerous aspects to consider and understand. As a product support expert, I’ve witnessed firsthand how the vast array of possibilities can leave customers feeling a bit lost.

To address this, I've created a comprehensive resource with @daniel_marlen designed to guide you through the intricacies of Central Procurement. After contributing to various wiki sites and knowledge-based articles (KBAs), I realized the need for a unified documentation that serves as a roadmap for understanding and utilizing Central Procurement effectively. The guide can be reached through the following KBA: 3544902 - Central Purchasing Troubleshooting - Guided Answers

Introducing: The Guided Answer Document for Central Procurement

The Guided Answer Document is a thoroughly crafted guide that consolidates all the essential information and processes related to Central Procurement. Here’s what you can expect:

With the Guided Answer Document, you no longer need to feel lost in the landscape of Central Procurement. This resource is designed to empower you, making your journey through centralized procurement processes smoother and more intuitive.

I encourage you to explore this comprehensive guide and make the most out of your SAP S/4HANA integration. Whether you are new to Central Procurement or a seasoned professional looking to optimize processes, this documentation will be an invaluable asset.

Stay tuned for updates and new insights as we continue to refine and expand this essential resource!

Best regards,
Tamás

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item [DP92]
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how Credit Management functions works in dynamic item processing.

The credit management function adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Dynamic Item Processor Framework (DIP) [transaction DP92], During this process a Debit Memo Request (DMR) document is generated. The DMR that is generated out of DIP could qualify for credit commitment update. If the predecessor transaction Service Order is Credit relevant, Hence Credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place.Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”.

Type of Credit Exposure Category

Name of Credit Exposure Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance types in service order for which credit management gets triggered. 

In this blog we will focusing more on Summarized Billing and Itemized Billing Processes.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is enabled for credit check.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enable.

Check the DIP Profile Setting: Transaction “ODP1” .

Summarized Billing Relevance with DCD Case:

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as “Summarized Billing” and enter the DIP Profile.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item. If the credit check is not successful, then service order will go into DCD case (Documented Credit Decisions). In this case user must go via approval process to get rid of the DCD blocker. Only authorized person can approve the DCD case for blocked service order depends upon the business.

Go to the “Manage Documented Credit decisions” app.

Enter the service order and click on release to approve the service order. Once the service order is approved then item will set it in “RELEASED” status.

Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit commitments exposure in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract) category.   

Check the credit commitment exposure in 110 category on service order released.

 

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item.

Manual release of the PMO in transaction via “IW32”.

 

Posting PMO Confirmations via IW48 transaction to create the Debit Memo Request. Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry. Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Click on Create Billing Request.

Debit Memo Request (DMR) is Created.

 

Check the credit commitment exposures. It should get updated in 410 category on DMR creation.

 

Create the Debit Memo.

 

As user created the debit memo, Hence credit commitment exposures will move from 410 to 500 category.

Check the 410 Commitment. As the 410 exposures moved to 500 category on successful creation of debit memo.

No value should persist in the 410 category.

Post the debit memo for accounting. Go to VF02 and Click on Release to Accounting flag.

Check the credit commitment exposures in 200 category

This is how credit exposures process work when there is DCD case.

Next we will learn about Itemized Billing.

Itemized Billing Relevance:

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item.

Manual release of the PMO in transaction IW32.

Posting PMO Confirmations in transaction IW48 to create the Debit Memo Request.

Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry.

Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Credit commitment exposures will get updated in 410 category on DMR creation.

Here credit commitments get calculated based on net value and taxes.

Create the Invoice after DMR creation.(Debit Memo)

Updating the 500 category upon successful creation of the invoice.

Removing the credit commitment exposures from 410 category.

Release the Invoice (Debit Memo) to accounting to move the credit commitments exposure to 200 category.

 

The distinct feature in this process flow is explained in detail how the credit management work in advanced execution order item in Service Order.

entry.title: Transforming Classic ABAP to ABAP Cloud: From Background Jobs to Application Jobs
entry.content:
Introduction: The Journey from Classic to Cloud

In the S/4HANA world, we hear about Clean Core all the time. Most of us also understand why Clean Core is such a great approach. And when we talk about S/4HANA Cloud, the Core is clean by definition.

However, I can imagine how inconvenient and counterintuitive resolving a general development task can be for a developer experienced in standard ABAP when working in the Cloud or Clean Core environment.

So, what am I sharing? In this blog, my intention is not to reiterate the principles of Clean Core, nor is it to provide detailed documentation of ABAP Cloud. Instead, I aim to show you an example of what happens when you need to solve a classic task in a new, cloud-ready way.

 

Classic ABAP: The Good, the Bad, and the Background Jobs

Any experienced ABAP developer knows what background jobs are. We use them all the time, especially when we want to run tasks periodically and automatically in the system.

When we need our custom code to execute periodically, we create a report with the required logic and schedule it as a background job step. You can refer to this blog to refresh your memory on how this is done.

There’s no doubt that even as we step into the Cloud and leave many obsolete methods behind, we still need such essential functionality. But wait - reports are not available in the Cloud!

Real life scenarios:

Imagine this: you are a seasoned SAP developer with several trusty ABAP reports for background usage that have worked perfectly for years. One day, you move to the Cloud, and you can’t take those reports with you!

Another case: you’re implementing all your developments in a Clean Core way from the beginning. You receive a requirement to code several processes and have them run autonomously in the system (e.g., a periodic data update). You know exactly how to solve it: create a report, schedule it in SM36 - but these tools are no longer available. You need a new way.

 

The Cloud Awakens: Modernising with ABAP Cloud

Good news! A new, cloud-ready method exists, and it comes in the beautiful class-based form we love in modern ABAP development.

How to do it, in a nutshell:

  1. Terminology Update: Instead of “Background Job,” you’ll now deal with an “Application Job.” The transition from SAP GUI to Fiori means the Application Jobs app is now your tool for scheduling. Learn more here.
  2. Code the Business Logic: Instead of creating a report, you’ll create a class. To make the class compatible with Application Jobs, add two interfaces: IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT and IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT. For SAP BTP ABAP Environment, different interfaces apply (see links below).
  3. Register Your Class: Register your class for Application Jobs by creating a Job Catalog Entry.
  4. Create a Job Template: To create a "variant" of your Job Catalog with prefilled fields, create a Job Template. All these steps are done in Eclipse ADT.

Once you’re finished, use the Application Jobs Fiori app to schedule a job based on the Job Template.

Profit:

Old school reports

->

Modern class-based implementation

SAP GUI: SE38, SM36, SM37

->

Eclipse native configurations & Application Jobs Fiori app

Classic development

->

Clean Core-compliant development with all its advantages

S/4HANA On Premise

->

 Any S/4HANA or BTP ABAP Environment

Let's see a real-life example.

 

Real-World Example: A Customer's Case

The problem.

One of my customers relied on a standard Analytical Query built on a complex stack of CDS views and AMDP procedures. The data selection speed was insufficient.

The solution.

We decided to persist the analytical data daily for quick access. On top of this persisted data, we created a new analytical view for high-performance data selection. To automate this process, we needed a periodic job.

Additional conditions.

The project strategy required Clean Core implementation.

Let’s see how this was achieved.

 

The How-To: Step-by-Step Guide

1. Class with business logic

Create an ABAP class for your application job:

 

Define parameters for the Application Job (e.g., using class constants)

See how this parameters will look like in the Fiori App:

We now need to define what happens, when the Application Job framework calls our class:

 
2. Job Catalog

We need to create an entry and specify the class for job execution - all in Eclipse ADT:

 
3. Job Template

Finally, we create a variant for our job. We can add values for some of the parameters:

 

Now we are all set from the development point of view. We can launch the Application Jobs app and create a job. 

 

P.S.

That’s it! The Application Jobs app includes additional features (e.g., job status monitoring) not covered in this blog. Refer to the official documentation for more.

For SAP BTP ABAP Environment, updated versions of class interfaces exist. At the time of writing, S/4HANA still uses the older versions.

This blog isn’t a comprehensive guide to all “Standard ABAP → ABAP Cloud” transformations but demonstrates how familiar concepts can be adapted for the Cloud.

I hope I’ve shown that there’s no rocket science here!

So, what’s next? Use this blog as a quick guide for ABAP Application Jobs or as inspiration for ABAP Cloud. Stay tuned for more on tackling challenges in the Classic ABAP → ABAP Cloud journey!

 

Links

  1. Application Jobs in ABAP Environment (BTP) (latest)
  2. Application Jobs in S/4HANA (latest)
  3. Application Jobs - Learning Journey

entry.title: Mass Processing in Resource Related Billing for Service with Advanced Execution Item
entry.content:
Mass Processing in Resource Related Billing for Service with Advanced Execution Item 

This blog will help you to understand how mass processing is done in the Resource Related Billing for Service. 

DP92 transaction is used as Resource Related Billing (RRB) solution for Service Order with Advanced Execution Items. DP92 will take Actual Costs from the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) into account and creates new Debit Memo Request (DMR) and / or Credit Memo Request (CMR) types. The account assignment object used on the service side is New Fin Item-Based accounting.  

Customer Service (CS) module supports massive generation of Debit Memo Requests (DMR) and / or Credit Memo Requests (CMR) via DP95 transaction Also earlier versions of RRB solution provide capability to process one Service Order Item at once 

 To overcome the limitation of processing single Service Order Item and to provide feature parity for existing CS customers a new feature is being enabled for processing multiple Service Orders with capability to generate one or many billing document requests for each of the service orders. The mass processing in resource related billing for advanced execution item has been introduced. 

New Transaction Code ‘DP92A’ has been created, along with that newly created report  "RVPKMASS_MCSV" will be called upon running DP92A transaction, this report will call the FM ‘DIPCOL_COLLECTIVE_MCSV_RRB’ to process the selected service order items.  

Selection Screen:  

Significance of each input field: 

Service Order 

Enter the Service Order Ex. 8000016785 

Service Order Item 

Enter the Service Order Item Ex. 00010 

Sales Organization 

Enter the Sales Organization Ex.1710 

Distribution Channel 

Enter the Distribution Channel Ex.10 

Division 

Enter the Division Ex.00 

Sales Office 

Enter sales office that is responsible for specific sales and distribution products and services.  Ex. O 50000231  

Sales Group 

Enter the Sales Group which is sales employees, responsible for processing specific products or services. Ex. SGrp001 

Created By 

Name of user who created the transaction. 

Posting Date 

The posting date contains the date on which the transaction took place. This date differs from the date of entry if transactions are not entered directly, but for example, entered in the system retroactively. 

Sold-To Party 

Enter the Sold-To-Party that is used in the Service Order Ex.17100001 

Bill-To Party 

Enter the Bill-To-Party that is used in the service order Ex. 17100002 

Payer 

Enter the Payer that is used in the service order Ex. CSM1000 

 
Cost Period Option: 

From Period 

Posting period when processing should begin and carried out. 

Fiscal Year From 

Fiscal year, in which processing should begin and be carried out. 

Posting Date To 

Date on which the  document was entered in financial accounting or cost accounting. 

Further Options: 

Use Saved Data 

This flag specifies whether data extract saved to be used or not. 

If the saved data is ‘X’ , In this case if no data is found from the extract, then it creates document/data extract from the search criteria provided  

Refresh  

Specifies whether the saved data for the object to be billed should be refreshed. If Saved data is ‘X’ and Refresh is ‘X’ then the system first reads the saved data and subsequently rereads the sources. 

Create Options: 

Save DMR/CMR Extract  

When Save DMR/CMR Extract is ‘X’. When selected report reads the selection criteria and process the same, Processed DIP line items are created as ‘saved data extract’.  

Save DMR/DMR Document 

When Save DMR/DMR Document is ’X’. Report reads the data from the search criteria provided by the user and generates sales documents.  

Processing Options: 

Test Run 

Processing type for programs which change data: 

  • When the flag is active: the program is executed normally, all checks in the program are performed, their results are output in various forms (as single message, as a collection of messages, or as a log), but existing data are not changed. 
  • When the flag is inactive: the program is executed normally. All data changes (change, delete, insert new data records) are made in the database (real run). 

Background Processing 

Specifies background processing 

Detail List 

Indicator “Generated detailed list”  

Parallel Processing 

Indicator “Parallel Processing” 

How to execute the Mass Processing for advanced execution order Item in RRB solution 

  • Enter the transaction DP92A in command prompt. 
  • Users will get below Input screen. Enter the set of service orders to create the DMR/CMR. 
  • Select “Save DMR/CMR Document” 
  • Test Run is Inactive. 
  • Select the Detail List. 
  • Click on execute. 

  • When the selected items processed successfully, report output will be displayed as below.  

In the output screen user can see the “Processed” and “Incorrect “category.

Select the processed category and click on detail list. User can view newly created DMR for each entered service order. 

If any error occurs while processing the selected items, report output will be displayed as below.   

Select the “Incorrect Category” and click on detail list to see the error message. User can see the error message for the item for which DMR/CMR not created. 

This is how user can successfully create the mass processing for DMR/CMR for advanced execution order items.

entry.title: Switching from ALV GUI to List Report Fiori RESTful Abap Programming is now easy
entry.content:
List report read-only 

In this topic will create a simple Fiori List Report without object page. It's recommended to use odata v4 but here we will create both v2 and v4

CDS Model 

The CDS model is driven by the VDM(Virtual data model) which follow consistent modeling and naming rules.

View entity Basics/Composites

Create new data definition language in ADT(need eclipse 😀 )

DDIC view based are obsoletes, so we only use view entity.

We don't use table directly beceause it's not clean core, instead we use release CDS, you can see this if they are flagged with "#PUBLIC_LOCAL_API" or release C1 contract in API State.

Now fill your new CDS :

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS basic purchase orders'
define view entity ZI_MyPurchaseOrders
  as select from I_PurchaseOrderAPI01
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'DocumentCurrency'
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency

}

 

Compile your CDS, and if you want you can add documentation :

Now you can fill your own documentation

And if you refresh your CDS you can open your documentation or if you press F2 on your object you can see documentation with definition

Now we will create our root view entity but you can also create other CDS levels between the two.

The root view entity is the last level before projection view. We'll go into more detail in the object section on page

You can use the code below

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS root purchase orders'
define root view entity ZR_MyPurchaseOrders
  as select from ZI_MyPurchaseOrders
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency
}

 

Compile

View entity Projection View

Now you need to create the top level view. Only the fields you have in this view can be displayed.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS Projection view Purchase Orders'
define root view entity ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS
  provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on ZR_MyPurchaseOrders
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency
}

 

As you're pointing to a root cds view, you need to specify the root instruction in the projection view too.

Also you need to provide a contract, for our example it's "transactional_query", you can bypass this but it's not recommended.

Compile

Metadata Extension

The metadata extension is used to arrange elements on the UI application with annotations. You can have multiple levels of metadata extension. 

Add fields position in table

By default, no fields will be displayed. To achieve this, we're going to add annotations at the CDS level (where they can also be added in the frontend application).

So firstly you need to add this annotation in your projection view

 

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

 

Then to add a field in first position you'll use this annotation

 

"""Metadata extension """
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 },{ importance: #HIGH }]

 

Increment from 10 to 10 to make reworking easier if you've got a position wrong.

Add selection fields(filters) position in table

 

"""Metadata extension """
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

 

Add value helps

To add value helps your have two options, one with relationship with foreign key and the second with cds view

We focus only on the second option. To do this, we'll need a cds view of type value help, which we'll call up and which will return all the values in the database.

 

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: '/DMO/I_AGENCY_STDVH', element: 'AgencyID'  } }]

 

Create and fill your metadata extension

Now that we have the three default annotations for a correct list report, we can get started.

Then you can choose to annotate entity or view.

Choose view for example

Now the aim is to add annotations for every field you want

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
@UI: {
    headerInfo: {
        typeName: 'Purchase Order',
        typeNamePlural: 'Purchase Orders'
    },
    presentationVariant: [{
        sortOrder: [{
            by: 'PurchaseOrder',
            direction: #DESC
        }],
        visualizations: [{
            type: #AS_LINEITEM
        }]
    }]
}
@Search.searchable: true
annotate view ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS with
{
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 },{ importance: #HIGH }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchaseOrder;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 },{ importance: #HIGH }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: 'I_PurchasingDocumentType', element: 'PurchasingDocumentType'  } }]
PurchaseOrderType;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 30 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchaseOrderSubtype;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchasingDocumentOrigin;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 50 }]
CreatedByUser;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 60 }]
CreationDate;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 70 }]
PurchaseOrderDate;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
CashDiscount1Percent;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 90 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 90 }]
CashDiscount1Days;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 100 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 100 }]
DownPaymentAmount;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 110 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 110 }]
DocumentCurrency;
}

 

Business Services

Now we will define entities and create odata service

Service Definition

Create new Service Definition

Insert the code below

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Service definition for ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
define service ZUI_MYPURCHORDERS {
  expose ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS as PurchaseOrders;
}

 

Don't forget to alias your entities

Service Binding

Now we'll create our odata service. Here's how to create an odata v2 and v4 service.

You need to activate your service binding (ctrl+f3)

Now you have two way to activate your odata service, use the publish button BUT it's only for testing because the input will be local and cannot be put into a package, or use the t-code /IWFND/V4_ADMIN to transport it in package.

I advise you to check that there are no errors with the publish option in service binding, as you can sometimes get errors with conversion routines (which are not allowed in view entities with 3 exceptions). Once ok, you can unpublish it and then republish it with the transaction for productive use.

Now your odata V4 is ready to use.

For V2 it's the same logic but for productivity you need to activate with t-code /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

 

Access control (DCL)

If you want to add authorization objects in this application, you can add access control. For example if you want restrict purchase order type with PFCG object. Once you've created your odata service, you'll need to add the authorization objects used in access control in su24.

Make sure you have enabled authorization control on the root view

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK

 

Create DCL(Access Control) for root view entity :

Then insert the code below

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Access control for ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS {
    grant 
        select
            on
                ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS
                    where ( PurchaseOrderType ) = aspect pfcg_auth ( M_BEST_BSA,BSART,actvt = '03' );
                        
}

 

And of course, if you want to use your own specific authorization object, that's also possible.

Now create another DCL(Access Control) for projection view. This DCL will only inherit the DCL of the root view.

Make sure you have enabled authorization control on the projection view

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK

 

Insert the below code

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Access control for ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS {
    grant
        select
            on
                ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS
                    where
                        inheriting conditions from entity ZR_MyPurchaseOrders;
                        
}

 

 

Now you just need to add yours authorizations objects in your odata service with t-code SU24, and create role in PFCG and assign to you or users.

Fiori Element

Open Business Application Studio or VScode(with configured Fiori tools extension).

Launch "Fiori open application generator" with command palette(ctrl+shift+p)

Select Fiori Element List report and select your system, odata service,project name, options

Now your app is ready to test.

You'll notice that by default it has added a navigation in manifest.json to an object page that we haven't coded in the CDS views. We'll set this aside.

    },
        "PurchaseOrdersObjectPage": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "PurchaseOrdersObjectPage",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "editableHeaderContent": false,
              "contextPath": "/PurchaseOrders"
            }
          }
        }

Test your app with command 

npm run start

You can also test your value help in purchase order type

And congrats, you've got a simple List Report application that respects the new development standards.

Of course, this display is very simplistic as we've added very few features, so feel free to dig deeper. You can also extend your application to add a custom filter or a new column. And if the design of the lists reports doesn't suit your needs you can always go for FPM and even freestyle(but no longer recommended 😀 )

 

Technical Catalog

If you want to deploy for an Private Cloud/On-Premise system in the Fiori Launchpad, you need to create at least a technical catalog. To do this create your catalog in t-code /UI2/FLPAM an fill your Fiori application technical data(no longer use launchpad designer please)

 

Next steps

The next step is to deploy your application on your SAP system. To do this, you'll need to run the deployment configuration (npm run deploy-config). Also you may need to fill in the object page and we'll look at that in a second part. And why not show how to add an action with behavior definition(BDEF).

entry.title: Transfer of payment card details from S/4 Service Order to DMR/CMR [Resource Related Billing - DP92]
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, an SCP app named “Payment Hub” introduced for digital payment card management. Payment Hub provides the interaction between SAP applications and payment service providers. SAP applications can use the payment hub to register, authorize, settle the payment cards by payment service providers, without saving sensitive data in the SAP systems.   

And “ in S4 HANA Service, the SAP digital payments add-on is integrated with service order processing. And it supports the SAP digital payments add-on for processing credit card payments.” 

During service processes, credit cards for one-time use are registered through the Payment Hub by a service provider. Once registered, a token is issued to the SAP system for operations such as authorization and settlement. 

  • If service processes use BDR (Billing Document Request) to complete their billing process, this token will be transferred to SAP FI.
  • In case of service sales integration, token is transferred to sales items via service order integration to sales order process. 
  • In S/4HANA Service, service confirmations generate Billing Request Documents (BDRs), which are used to create billing documents. In such use cases, payment card data and authorizations from service confirmations are copied into BDRs and billing documents. 
  • SAP systems store only card tokens, showing limited details like the masked card number and cardholder name. In service processes, cards are authorized automatically for billing-relevant items. Settlement and advice are managed by SAP FI. 

After billing document creation: 

  • Authorization status is set to “Used,” and the billing document ID is saved in the authorization data. 
  • Authorizations cannot be updated anymore. 
  • If a billing document is cancelled, the authorization status reverts to “Open”, and the Billing Document ID is removed.  

For more detailed information about the integration of SAP digital payments add-on with service order processing. Please refer the link.

This section of the blog explains how payment card details are transferred from the advance execution order items summarized [F] or Itemized [G] billing relevance to the DMR/CMR document during resource related billing process [Transaction DP92]. 

During resource related billing process, payment cards registered via service orders are replicated to Debit/Credit Memo Request (DMR/CMR). The DMR/CMR handles authorization for the open amount and generates new authorizations for partial billings based on existing SD logic. Further the authorizations in the DMR/CMR are maintained independently and are not returned to the service order. 

Points to be remember:  

  • Payment card details cannot be deleted from the service order if linked to any DMR/CMR. 
  • Payment card integration feature is currently supported for payer maintained on service order header level. 

     Integration of payment card with AEO item with summarized or itemized billing relevance.

Prerequisites: 

  1. Connection to Payment Hub 
    • Payment Hub is an Add-on product of S4/HANA. In the SAP Note 2524512, the details for connecting payment hub are described, like RFC connection, creation of card type, activation of payment hub, and activation of scenarios.  
    • Payment Scenario 05 will be used for AEO items (Billing Rel. Summarized/Itemized). Through scenario ‘05’, the usage of payment hub is activated additionally for SD sales order. If scenario is not activated, the ‘Payment Method’ assignment block will not appear in service transactions. 

     2. Activate Payment Card for Service Transaction 

  • Create checking group for payment cards. 

Customer management uses the same checking group for payment card as Sales and Distribution.  

SPRO: Sales and Distribution->Billing->Payment Cards->Authorization and Settlement->Maintain Checking Groups 

  • Assign payment plan type and checking group to service transaction type.  

 Payment plan type ‘03’ is defined system widely for payment cards.  

 SPRO: Customer Management->Transactions->Basic Settings->Define Transaction Types (under the transaction category ‘Sales’ the assignment can be done.) 

Process Flow 

The following steps outline the process flow for transferring payment card details from service order (AEO item (Billing Rel. Summarized/Itemized)) to the DMR/CMR document during the resource-related billing process via DP92: 

1. Create a Service Order having an execution item. 

 2. Navigate to the item overview page. Scroll to the Billing assignment block, change the Billing Relevance to "Summarized Billing," and assign a DIP profile. 

3. Navigate to the Payment Card assignment block on the service order page while in edit mode. Use the "Register Payment Card" button to register a payment card. 

Note: To use a card linked to the payer, select "Add Existing Card."  

4.  Enter the card details and click on submit. 

After successful submission, a dialog box will display the message: “Your request has been sent to the service provider. The screen will be closed now.” 

Click on Ok button and close the pop-up. You can see payment card has been added to “Payment Cards” assignment block.

5.  Release the advance execution item. 

Note:  After releasing the AEO item with Billing Relevance set to "Summarized," you can verify that no authorization has been generated under payment card section, as expected.

Also, in the “Transaction History” section, verify the generation of the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO)

6. Launch “Change Maintenance Order” application through launchpad and release the Plant Maintenance Order (PMO). Then save it. 

7. Launch “Enter PM Order Confirmation” application and create confirmation corresponding to Plant Maintenance Order. 

8. Then the next step is to Create Debit Memo Request through app “Create Resource-Related Billing Request – Service Order” [DP92].

 Enter Service Order and service order item. Click on “Billing Document Request” button.       

You can verify that a DMR document has been created, containing the payment card details assigned to the Service Order, without any authorization. 

9. To create an authorization for the DMR document, ensure the document is completed and saved without any errors. Once saved successfully, the authorization will be created automatically, as demonstrated below. 

This ensures the successful transfer of payment card details from Service Order to Debit Memo Request during the resource related billing process.

entry.title: Credit Check Behaviour for Unplanned Items in Service Processes
entry.content:
In service industries, unplanned items (such as additional parts, unexpected labour, expenses or services) often arise during the execution of a service order. These items might arise due to unforeseen complications or new customer requests, which weren’t anticipated during the service order creation. Managing the financial impact of these unplanned items is critical to maintaining healthy cash flow and managing customer relationships. Understanding how these items interact with the credit management process is crucial for both businesses and customers. 

Overview of credit management in service orders 

Credit management is an integral part of service management, particularly for ensuring that services rendered are financially covered and reducing the risk of payment defaults. The credit check functionality in service processes is typically triggered at the service order level, not during the service confirmation phase. This check assesses whether the customer’s available credit is sufficient to cover the cost of the service order. 

If the credit check passes successfully, the system moves forward by updating the credit exposure against the service order’s payer partner’s credit segment (under 110 category). However, in the case where the credit check fails, a Documented Credit Decision (DCD) case is generated for review. 

For unplanned items, the process is different. These items are not subject to a direct credit check. Instead, their presence only leads to an update in credit exposure during the follow-up service process i.e. during service confirmation phase. Below, we will delve into the details of how unplanned items affect credit exposure and how the system handles these scenarios. 

How unplanned item interact with credit checks? 

Unlike planned service items that go through a credit check as part of the service order processing, unplanned items are handled differently. The key differences are as follows: 

  1. Credit Check for Service Orders: 
  • The credit check is triggered on the service order and not on the service confirmation. 
  • If the credit check for the service order is successful, the system will proceed by updating the credit exposure against the respective payer’s credit segment. 
  • If the credit check fails for the service order, the system generates a Documented Credit Decision (DCD) case, which is typically a manual review process. 

    2. No Credit Check for Unplanned Items: 

  • For unplanned items, no credit check is performed. These items are not factored into the credit check process during service order processing. 
  • In this case only credit exposure is updated based on the unplanned item’s gross price. Since this unplanned item is added in the service confirmation stage, the credit exposure is updated under 410 category 

Conditions for updating credit exposure corresponding to the service confirmation unplanned item (under 410 category) 

Before updating credit exposure in the 410 categories for the service confirmation, the system performs several checks to ensure the update is valid and accurate: 

  1. Is Credit Management Enabled in the System? 

The system verifies whether credit management is enabled for the relevant service processes. Without active credit management, no credit exposure updates, nor credit checks can take place. 

    2. Is the Unplanned Item in the Service Confirmation Billing Relevant? 

The unplanned item must be billing relevant in the service confirmation. Only those unplanned items that are intended to be billed can influence the credit exposure update. 

   3. Is the Item Category of the Unplanned Item in the Service Order Relevant for Credit Check? 

The system checks whether the item category of the unplanned item is defined as credit check relevant in the service order. If it is not marked as relevant for credit checks, it will not impact the credit exposure. 

   4. Is the Transaction Type of the Preceding Service Order Relevant for Credit Check? 

The system verifies that the transaction type for the preceding service order is relevant for a credit check. If the transaction type is not credit check relevant, the unplanned item will not affect the credit exposure. 

   5. Does the Payer in the Service Confirmation Have an Assigned Credit Segment Role? 

The system checks if the payer in the service confirmation has a valid credit segment role. Without a credit segment role, the system will not update the credit exposure for the payer. 

Process flow 

The following steps outline the process flow for Credit Check Behaviour for Unplanned Items in Service Processes: 

  1. Create a service order with a service item. 

You can verify that the current credit status for a planned item is "Relevant for credit check, but credit check not executed." 

    2. Release the service item. 

   3. Verify Credit Status of Service Order Item. 

After releasing the service item, check the credit status of the service order item. It should be “Credit Check Ok,” meaning the credit check for the planned items has passed successfully. 

Correspondingly, the credit exposure will be updated in the 110 categories for the service order. 

     4. Create a service confirmation corresponding to the previously created service order. 

    5. Add an unplanned item through “Add unplanned item button”. 

     6. Complete the service confirmation unplanned item. 

     7. As soon as you completed the service confirmation, an unplanned item will be added to service order automatically with credit status “Not relevant for credit check”. 

       

   8. Verify Credit Exposure in 110 Categories for Service Order Unplanned Item. 

 As expected, the service order unplanned item will not have any credit exposure under 110 categories. 

   9. Verify the credit exposure in 410 categories for service confirmation unplanned item. 

The system will update the credit exposure under 410 categories for the unplanned items that were added to the service confirmation. 

This process ensures that no credit check is performed for unplanned item, either in the service order or in service confirmation. The only action taken is the update of credit exposure for the unplanned items in the 410 categories under the service confirmation. 

Conclusion 

The credit check process for unplanned items in service orders is designed to balance operational efficiency with financial oversight. This approach provides flexibility in dealing with unpredictable service needs, while still safeguarding the business from potential financial risks, ensuring a smooth experience for both businesses and customers.

entry.title: How to: Adding Product Hierarchy to analytical CDS Views
entry.content:
In the following post, I will show you how to extend the CDS version of the Fiori App: Market Segments - Actuals and Market Segments - Plan/Actual. This can be, however, used for any CDS View if you know the name of the CDS view. 

Please change the naming of the sqlViewAppendName and the WITH statement based on your naming convention, as every name must be unique.

First of all, you need to extend this CDS View E_JournalEntryItem and E_FinancialPlanningEntryItem:

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXEJOURNALITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for View E_JournalEntryItem'
extend view E_JournalEntryItem with ZXX_JOURNALENTRYITEM_PRODHPA
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_SalesOffice as _SalesOffice on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOffice.SalesOffice
association [0..1] to I_SalesOfficeText as _SalesOfficeText on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOfficeText.SalesOfficeName
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  Persistence.prodh_pa as prodh_pa,
  
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_SalesOffice'
  Persistence.vkbur_pa as vkbur_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText,
  _SalesOffice,
  _SalesOfficeText
}

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXEJOURNALITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for View E_FinancialPlanEntIt'
extend view E_FinancialPlanningEntryItem with ZXX_E_Financialplanningentr
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  Persistence.prodh_pa as prodh_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText
}

 

 

This way, you will enable it for the I_JournalEntryItemCube and I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube. However, these two need to be extended as well. The first extension was just to propagate the field.

Now you need to do these two extensions for I_JournalEntryItemCube and I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube:

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXIJRNLENTITCU'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension of I_JournalEntryItemCube of Prodh_PA/VKBUR_PA'
extend view I_JournalEntryItemCube with  ZXX_I_JournaEntryItCuProdHier
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
association [0..1] to I_SalesOffice as _SalesOffice on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOffice.SalesOffice
association [0..1] to I_SalesOfficeText as _SalesOfficeText on $projection.vkbur_pa = _SalesOfficeText.SalesOfficeName
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
  _Extension.prodh_pa,
  
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_SalesOffice'
  _Extension.vkbur_pa,

  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText,
  _SalesOffice,
  _SalesOfficeText
}
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXJRNITMCUBE'
@EndUserText.label: 'Journal Entry cube extension'
extend view I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube with ZXX_PRODH_PA
association [0..1] to I_ProductHierarchy as _ProductHierarchy on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchy.ProductHierarchy
association [0..*] to I_ProductHierarchyText as _ProductHierarchyText
    on $projection.prodh_pa = _ProductHierarchyText.ProductHierarchy
{
   .textArrangement: #TEXT_FIRST
    @AbapCatalog.compiler.caseJoin
    @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_ProductHierarchy'
   case 
    when APJEI.ActualPlanCode ='A'
        then _Extension_acdoca.prodh_pa
    when APJEI.ActualPlanCode ='P'
        then _Extension_acdocp.prodh_pa
   end as prodh_pa,
  _ProductHierarchy,
  _ProductHierarchyText
}

 

With this you are now able to use both fields in the cubes. As the last step you will extend the consumption CDS view. 

This extension is for C_MarketSegmentsQ2501 which is Market Segments - Actuals.

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXMARKETPLAN'
@EndUserText.label: 'market segment ext'
extend view C_MarketSegmentQ2501  with ZFO_E_MarketSegmentQ2501
{
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  prodh_pa,
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT
  vkbur_pa
    
}

 

 

 

 

 This extension is for C_MarketSegmentsPlanActQ2501 which is Market Segments - Plan/Actuals.

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXMARKETACTPLAN'
@EndUserText.label: 'Market Segments - Plan/Actuals - Ext PRODH_PA'
extend view C_MarketSegmentPlanActQ2501 with ZXX_E_MarketSegmentsPlanAct

{
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #FREE
@AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
@AnalyticsDetails.query.display: #KEY_TEXT

prodh_pa

}

 

When you have all these extensions in your system you will see the Product Hierarchy with description in the query, if selected.

Result of extensions in Market Segments - Actuals

entry.title: Thoughts About Generative AI in Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Reciprocal Relationship Between Master Data Governance and Generative Artificial Intelligence

In this blog post, I'd like to point out how generative AI impacts master data management and vice versa.

Master Data Governance Facilitating Generative Artificial Intelligence

In simple terms, generative AI is meant to automatically create reliable output in response to user prompts. With regards to master data governance there are multiple dimensions to this. On the one hand, master data governance is the foundation that ensures clean, consistent, and reliable master data, which is crucial for fact-based decision-making and flawless business transactions across organizations. Such a foundation is also pivotal in an AI context. Any generative AI output depends on the quality of the underlying source data from large language models (LLM). In a business context, training data is often based on corporate data, including master data. Poor data quality can lead to so-called AI hallucinations (like what is known as "garbage in/garbage out" from an analytical perspective). This means in a nutshell that the accuracy of LLM-derived responses depends heavily on the quality of the prompts and the underlying data quality itself. This simply means that the importance of effective master data governance becomes even more relevant when generative AI is applied in a business context, in which master data provides a critical foundation. To find out more about the role of master data governance in an AI context, read this article or listen to this podcast.

Generative Artificial Intelligence Facilitating Master Data Governance 

On the other hand, state-of-the art master data governance must be able to cope with challenges such as processing large data volumes with as little manual intervention as possible. And the good thing is that SAP Master Data Governance features various means to this end already, for example:

  • ML-based rule mining for the automated detection of hidden patterns in master data and the resulting proposal and definition of additional data quality rules
  • Derivation rules for automated field population
  • Data provider integration for the automated take-over of proven source data

Now, with SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, the use of generative AI is an additional capability to support automation and consumer-grade user experience.

Note: You may also watch the SAP let’s talk data podcast about the value of automation in MDM (with Andreas Seifried)

Generative AI in central governance (classic mode)

Generative AI is applied in central governance (classic mode) for summary of changes and assisted changes. To get an idea of how this works, watch the short video:

Video showing gen AI applied in central governance (classic mode)

 

To watch the video, click on the picture above. 

Generative AI in business partner processing (cloud-ready mode)

In addition, generative AI can be used in business partner processing (cloud-ready mode) initiated by user prompts using the SAP Joule copilot.

User prompts in Joule copilot and follow-ups in MDG

 This integration enables:

  • User prompts and dialog in natural language with AI copilot Joule to get details on business partner data.
  • Option to directly call MDG for follow-up processing in cloud-ready mode.

The AI features described in this blog post are only available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (not in the on-premise version).

As pointed out before, the impact of a consistent master data layer for generative AI based output is huge while the journey of AI integration in SAP Master Data Governance processes has only just begun. There is more to come. Stay tuned. 

Best,
Markus

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Intelligence: SAP Business AI, Joule, and Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a crucial driving force for innovation and growth. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, offers a suite of AI solutions tailored to meet the diverse needs of modern businesses. Among these, SAP Business AI stands out, with two primary offerings:  Joule and Embedded AI. This blog explores the distinctions between these two powerful solutions, highlighting their unique features and capabilities.

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

What is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI represents a collection of intelligent technologies designed to automate and optimize business processes within the enterprise environment. By leveraging machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced AI techniques, SAP Business AI enables organizations to make data-driven decisions faster.

Picture1: Key features of SAP Business AI

Key Features of SAP Business AI

  1. Embedded AI: SAP embeds AI capabilities directly into its existing solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP C/4HANA, and SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring seamless user experience and quick adoption.
  2. Automated Machine Learning: SAP Business AI leverages automated machine learning to build and train models rapidly, without the need for extensive data science expertise.
  3. Natural Language Processing (NLP): It uses NLP to enable conversational AI, allowing users to interact with SAP systems using natural language.
  4. Robotic Process Automation (RPA): SAP Business AI employs RPA to automate repetitive tasks, freeing up human resources for more strategic work.
  5. Predictive Analytics: It offers predictive analytics to help businesses anticipate future trends, customer behavior, and market changes.

This blog is going to focus on Joule and Embedded AI and how they are used in SAP S/4HANA to improve user experience and make product extensions easier and more efficient.

Understanding Joule

Joule is a platform that allows businesses to develop, deploy, and manage AI applications with ease. It serves as a comprehensive toolbox for AI and Machine Learning (ML), providing developers with the necessary resources to build intelligent solutions tailored to specific business needs. Key features of Joule include:

  1. Flexible Development: Joule offers a versatile environment for developing AI models using preferred tools and frameworks. This flexibility empowers developers to create custom AI solutions that seamlessly integrate with existing systems.
  2. Scalability: The platform supports scalable deployment of AI applications, ensuring that businesses can handle increasing volumes of data and user requests efficiently.
  3. Ecosystem Compatibility: Joule is designed to work well with various SAP and third-party applications, fostering a collaborative ecosystem where different tools and technologies can interoperate.

In the ever-evolving landscape of enterprise resource planning, SAP S/4HANA has emerged as a powerful solution, offering a wide range of functionalities to streamline business processes. However, navigating through its complex interface can sometimes be challenging for users. This is where Joule comes into play. Joule is an innovative tool designed to simplify navigation in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing user experience and productivity.

Joule is a user-friendly navigation tool that helps users quickly find and access the required transactions, reports, and other functionalities within SAP S/4HANA. It acts as a search engine within the SAP environment, making it easier for users to locate specific features without having to memorize transaction codes or navigate through multiple menus.

 Key Features of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (I don’t know if you want to include Private as well – if yes, keep it as SAP S/4HANA Cloud)

  1. Intuitive Search: Joule allows users to perform keyword-based searches. Whether you're looking for a specific transaction code, report, or even a custom SAP program, Joule can find it for you in a matter of seconds.
  2. Smart Suggestions: As you type in the search bar, Joule provides smart suggestions based on your input. This feature significantly reduces the time spent searching for the right option, enhancing overall efficiency.
  3. Personalized Experience: Joule learns from your usage patterns and adapts to your preferences. It keeps track of frequently used transactions and reports, making them easily accessible from the home screen.
  4. Integrated Navigation: Joule integrates seamlessly with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud interface, providing a unified navigation experience. This integration ensures that users can access all necessary functionalities without having to switch between different tools or interfaces.
  5. Accessibility: Joule is accessible from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud home screen, making it convenient for users to start their search right from the main interface. This eliminates the need to open separate applications or modules.

Benefits of Using Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Improved Productivity: With Joule, users can quickly find what they need, reducing the time spent on navigation. This leads to increased productivity and efficiency.
  2. Reduced Training Time: New users can easily adapt to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with Joule's intuitive interface. The tool's simplicity and user-friendliness reduce the learning curve, making training more efficient.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Joule provides a seamless and personalized experience, tailored to each user's needs. This enhances overall satisfaction and makes the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system more approachable.
  4. Better Decision Making: By making it easier to access reports and analytics, Joule helps users make informed decisions promptly. This is particularly beneficial for managers and executives who need quick access to specific data.

Steps to Use Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access Joule: From the SAP S/4 HANA home screen, locate the Joule search bar. This is usually placed in a prominent position for easy access.
  2. Enter Keywords: Type in the keywords related to the transaction, report, or functionality you are looking for. As you type, Joule will provide relevant suggestions.
  3. Select the Desired Option: From the list of suggestions, select the desired option. Joule will navigate you directly to the relevant screen or transaction.

The screen shot below shows an example of how Joule can be used to quickly find information in your S/4HANA Cloud system. In this case the user wants to know how to create a Sales Order in the system.  Instead of having to navigate through the help menus the user can simply type “How do I create a sales order” in the Joule editor and Joule will display all the information necessary for the user to get started.

 

Figure 2: Sample of navigation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Joule

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

Figure 3: Form before the changes

 

Figure 4: Form after the changes

Conclusion

SAP Business AI offers a powerful suite of tools to help businesses achieve their digital transformation goals. Joule provides a flexible platform for easy navigation and fast data retrieval.  Joule can also be used to develop and deploy custom AI solutions, catering to the needs of developers and data scientists. In contrast, Embedded AI focuses on delivering ready-to-use AI functionalities integrated within existing SAP applications, providing immediate value to end-users.

Joule and Embedded AI can be used concurrently to simplify the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system by allowing users to retrieve data and perform operations faster and more efficiently.

For organizations looking to build custom AI applications, Joule also offers unparalleled flexibility and scalability. On the other hand, Embedded AI is ideal for businesses seeking quick, out-of-the-box AI solutions to enhance their existing SAP environments.

Ultimately, both Joule and Embedded AI play crucial roles in the SAP Business AI ecosystem, empowering businesses to unlock the full potential of artificial intelligence and drive sustainable growth.

Stay tuned for more insights into the evolving world of enterprise AI and its transformative impact on modern business operations.

entry.title: Billing Relevance G in execution order items in Service with Advanced Execution
entry.content:
The concept of Billing Relevance in Service with Advanced Execution is already explained in a previous blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/billing-relevance-in-execution-order-item/ba-p/13574529

This blog is to showcase the Service with Advanced execution process flow with billing relevance which is Itemized Billing (Key = G). This billing method is only available with advanced execution.

Itemized Billing is a billing scenario which uses the dynamic item processor (DIP) to bill services. This is a variant of resource-related billing which means customers are charged based on actuals.

In general, the price for tailor-made services can neither be defined in a service contract nor in the pricing master.

Typical examples of this are:

  • Industries which generally charge on actuals,
  • Specific services, such as consulting

In these cases, customers are billed for the resources consumed (for example, personnel, material, travel) to carry out the services. In resource-related billing, the system creates dynamic items from the cost information on the maintenance order.

The following steps depicts the process flow for Itemized Billing:

1. Create a service order and add an execution order item.

Service Order with an execution order item

2. Navigate to the item overview page in Edit mode, scroll down to the Billing assignment block and change Billing Relevance to Itemized Billing and enter a DIP Profile.

The dynamic item processor (DIP) profile is mandatory in the case of resource-related billing. During billing, the dynamic item processor determines the items or lines in the debit memo request, from the costs on the maintenance order, based on the DIP Profile entered in the execution order item in the service order.

Note: The DIP Profile entered in the service order must be assigned with Usage 1 Billing.

Billing assignment block in an execution order item

3. Release the item and save the service order.

Item status after creation of maintenance order

Maintenance order is generated, and the execution status of execution order item is set to Execution Planning.

4. Release the maintenance order after planning, to start execution.

Releasing the maintenance order

5. Create PM confirmations using Fiori app ‘Enter PM Order Confirmation’.

Enter PM Order Confirmation app

6. Fill in the actual work hours taken for the execution of operations and save.

PM Confirmation

Optionally, you can use the Final Confirmation checkbox to indicate that this is the last posting of actuals for an operation.

Final PM Confirmation

7. Open Post Goods Movement Fiori app to post goods issue for the actual components and quantities used.

Goods Issue

8. Open the Create Resource-Related Billing Request - Service Order app or you can use the transaction code DP92 to launch the screen in SAP GUI. Enter the service order ID and the execution order item. Then click Create.

Create Resource- Related Billing Request app

The system displays costs as shown below. The material determination is performed using the DIP profile and actuals are displayed from the data on the maintenance order. The actuals can be seen either in the form of amounts or quantities.

You can set the quantity or the amount to be billed. You can also reject or postpone partially by quantity or amount.

Billing request: Expense view

The prices are determined using SD Pricing and this is shown in Sales Price View.

Billing Request: Sales Price View

9. Select the Billing Request button to generate a debit memo request (DMR).

Create Billing Request

Debit Memo Request in Fiori app

Debit Memo Request

This is another view of the same Debit Memo Request which shows the item categories of the items. L2S here represents a statistical item whereas L2W will be billable. This is the distinct behavior with itemized billing – the main item, which is item 10, will be statistical and the subitems will be considered to calculate net value.

10. The Transfer to Billing is set as Successful and DMR references are updated in the Billing and Transaction History assignment blocks respectively in the execution order item overview page.

Billing assignment block after DMR creation

Transaction history assignment block after DMR creation

11. Open the Create Billing Documents app and search using the Debit Memo Request ID. Select the document from the search result and choose Create Billing Documents.

Create Billing Documents app

Lastly, you can post the Billing Document to accounting, to complete the process.

Post Billing Document

12. Open the Billing assignment block in execution order item again, you can see that the Billing Status is updated as Partially billed.

Billing assignment block after invoice creation
